Observability: TARS displays weather summary to pilot.
Directability: Pilot can command TARS to repeat or skip an item.
Information Requirement: METAR data, temperature, wind speed and direction, visibility, runway condition, icing risk.
Callout: Starting crew briefing - Weather: Temperature five degrees celsius, fog, reduced visibility expected. Wind zero niner zero degrees at four knots, light crosswind from the right for runway zero six left. Runway dry, no contamination reported. No gusts, no thunderstorms, no wind shear reports.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will announce and display the weather briefing summary
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display the weather briefing summary

Observability: TARS displays aircraft type and configuration summary to pilot.
Directability: Pilot can command TARS to repeat or skip an item.
Information Requirement: Aircraft type, engine type, MEL status, takeoff configuration.
Callout: Aircraft: Cessna Citation Mustang, light twin engine jet. No MEL items, no tech log issues affecting departure.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will announce and display the aircraft briefing summary
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display the aircraft briefing summary

Observability: TARS displays NOTAM summary to pilot.
Directability: Pilot can command TARS to repeat or skip an item.
Information Requirement: Active NOTAMs, runway status, navaid status, taxiway and lighting status.
Callout: NOTAMs: No departure critical NOTAMs affecting runway zero six left or initial climb. No reported navaid outages affecting departure routing. Taxiways and lighting operational.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will announce and display the NOTAMs briefing summary
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display the NOTAMs briefing summary

Observability: TARS displays departure routing and waypoints to pilot.
Directability: Pilot can command TARS to repeat or correct routing.
Information Requirement: Departure runway, initial heading, cleared altitude, waypoints, destination, engine-out plan.
Callout: Routing : Departure runway zero six left, montreal trudeau airport. Maintain runway heading after takeoff. Initial climb to five thousand feet. Waypoints OMEKI then left turn direct Ottawa Macdonald airport, plan arrival runway zero seven.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will announce and display the routing briefing summary
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display the routing briefing summary

Observability: TARS displays planned automation strategy to pilot.
Directability: Pilot can command TARS to adjust automation plan.
Information Requirement: Takeoff mode, autopilot engagement altitude, speed and heading modes, navigation plan.
Callout: Automation: Manual takeoff. After safe altitude fifteen hundred feet A G L reached, autopilot can be engaged
TARS Performer Role: TARS will announce and display the automation briefing summary
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display the automation briefing summary

Observability: TARS displays miscellaneous items and abnormality plan to pilot.
Directability: Pilot can command TARS to repeat or clarify items.
Information Requirement: Passenger count, abnormality plan before and after V1, cabin brief status.
Callout: Miscellaneous briefing. Single passenger onboard. Abnormality plan, before V1 reject takeoff. After V1 continue, maintain single engine climb speed one hundred and twenty knots, climb to one thousand five hundred feet, then autopilot and handle checklists. Crew briefing completed.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will announce and display the miscellaneous briefing summary
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display the miscellaneous briefing summary

Observability: TARS displays pilot's checklist progress; Pilotconfirms ATC clearance status.
Directability: Pilot can command TARS to recheck an item.
Information Requirement: ATC clearance status, checklist completion status.
Callout: Cessna Charlie Papa Oscar Lima Yankee cleared for takeoff, will maintain runway heading, climb to five thousand feet.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will readback the takeoff clearance and display the cleared parameters
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display received clearance and prepopulate parameters such as selected altitude in future task

Observability: TARS displays flap position to pilot; if performer TARS announces intention then sets flaps to takeoff position.
Predictability: Pilot anticipates TARS's flap setting callout before takeoff.
Directability: Pilot can confirm or override TARS's flap setting.
Information Requirement: Flap handle position, takeoff configuration (15°).
Callout: Flaps check - setting flaps for takeoff fifteen degrees
TARS Performer Role: TARS will announce intention then set flaps to takeoff position fifteen degrees then confirm done
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display flap setting reminder for takeoff check

Observability: TARS must display sensed pitot-static status to pilot.
Information Requirement: Pitot-static switch status
TARS Performer Role: TARS will check if pitot heat is on then continue to next item
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display sensed pitot-static switch position

Observability: TARS displays sensed engine anti-ice status changes to pilot and requirement.
Information Requirement: Outside air temperature, engine anti-ice switch position.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will suggest action

Observability: TARS displays windshield anti-ice status and requirement
Predictability: TARS alerts pilot if anti-ice status deviates from requirement.
Information Requirement: Windshield anti-ice switch position, current weather conditions.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will suggest action

Observability: TARS display PAX SAFETY switch status to pilot.
Information Requirement: PAX SAFETY switch position, checklist status.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will check if PAX safety switch is set to PAX safety then continue to next item
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display sensed Pax Safety switch position and continue to next item when sensed

Observability: TARS must display sensed light switch status.
Predictability: Pilot expects TARS to suggest if it is suggested based on time of day.
Information Requirement: Current runway conditions, weather, and aircraft status.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will suggest action

Observability: TARS display ANTI-COLL light switch status to pilot.
Predictability: Pilot expects TARS to alert if ANTI-COLL light switch is off.
Information Requirement: ANTI-COLL light switch position, checklist status.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will check if Anti-coll Lights Switch is set to ON then continue to next item
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display sensed Anti-coll Lights switch position and continue to next item

Information Requirement: EICAS visible

Observability: TARS displays and announce available wind data and status. Pilot can enrich by crosschecking with visual information
Directability: Both can perform in parallel and crosscheck
Information Requirement: Wind speed, direction, runway alignment.
Callout: Wind report from METAR : wind calm, 090 degrees at 4 knots
TARS Performer Role: TARS will announce and display available wind data from METAR, ATIS and takeoff clearance and will compute crosswind component
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display available wind data from METAR, ATIS and takeoff clearance and will compute crosswind component

Information Requirement: ATC clearance
TARS Performer Role: TARS will preset altitude as cleared
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display heard altitude clearance

Information Requirement: CAS status, system alerts, checklist progress.

Observability: The performer must callout the action before executing it and after execution
Information Requirement: Throttle position, engine parameters, runway conditions.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will recap the takeoff speeds and its role (e.g. I will announce airspeed's alive, v1, etc...) will sense and continue to next item

Observability: TARS displays FADEC status changes to pilot.
Directability: TARS direct human to RTO if FADEC fails
Information Requirement: FADEC status, engine parameters, TARS action log.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will check than callout FADEC status and continue to next item
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display FADEC status and continue to next item

Observability: Human sees TARS status on engine checks; TARS sees human's manual inputs.
Directability: TARS direct human to RTO if engine spool is not even
Information Requirement: Engine RPM, temperature, pressure, and spool rate.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will check than callout Engine spool and continue to next item
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display engine spool and continue to next item

Observability: TARS must display airspeed. Pilot must be able to hear announcement
Predictability: Pilot must anticipate TARS's "Airspeed's alive" announcement timing.
Information Requirement: Current airspeed, runway conditions, aircraft weight.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will callout "Airspeed's alive" and continue to next item
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will sense and continue to the next item

Observability: TARS must display airspeed; Human must hear TARS' "70 kts" announcement.
Predictability: Human expects TARS to announce "70 kts" at correct speed.
Information Requirement: Airspeed, current phase of takeoff.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will callout "70 kts" and continue to next item

Observability: TARS must display airspeed; Human must hear TARS' "V1" announcement.
Predictability: TARS announces "V1" timing based on takeoff speed and runway conditions.
Information Requirement: Current airspeed, runway length, aircraft weight, wind conditions.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will callout "V1" and continue to next item
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display V1 value and continue to next item

Observability: TARS must display airspeed; Human must hear TARS' "ROTATE" announcement.
Predictability: TARS announces "ROTATE" timing based on takeoff speed and runway conditions.
Information Requirement: Airspeed, runway length, aircraft weight, wind conditions.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will callout "Rotate"

Information Requirement: Current pitch angle, target pitch, airspeed, altitude.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will callout "pitch angle low" if the pitch is inferior to 10° then continue to next item

Observability: The performer must announce the result of the check and the subsequent action
Information Requirement: Current climb rate, target climb rate, altitude, airspeed.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will check climb rate and callout "Positive rate" then continue to next item
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will continue to the next item when positive climb rate is reached

Observability: TARS confirms pilot's gear action.
Information Requirement: Gear position, airspeed, altitude, climb rate.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will callout "GEAR UP"

Observability: TARS displays agreed safe altitude (1500ft)
TARS Supporter Role: TARS announce safe altitude then continue to next item

Observability: TARS displays current flight director mode and status changes to pilot.
Predictability: TARS communicates intended flight director mode changes before execution.
Directability: Pilot can override or confirm TARS's flight director mode changes via voice command.
Information Requirement: Current flight director mode, engine status, autopilot engagement.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will announce then set Flight Director to TakeOff mode then continue to next item

Information Requirement: Current pitch angle, engine status, autopilot engagement.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will callout "pitch angle low" if the pitch is inferior to 10° then continue to next item

Observability: TARS displays gear status and alerts pilot of any anomalies.
Information Requirement: Engine status, gear position, airspeed, altitude.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will check L/G status and announce result then continue to next item

Observability: TARS displays current airspeed and V2 status to pilot.
Information Requirement: Current airspeed, V2 speed, engine status.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will check airspeed compared to V2 and remind to target it if necessary then continue to next item
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display V2 value then continue to next item

Observability: TARS displays rudder trim status. TARS display its own adjustments, TARS announce each trim input
Predictability: TARS announces intended rudder trim adjustments before execution. TARS always execute the equivalent of a single input on the electric trim
Directability: Pilot can override or adjust TARS rudder trim actions via voice command.
Information Requirement: Engine status, current rudder trim position, airspeed, and yaw rate.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will prompt and wait for approval to trim rudder, TARS will announce trim direction then trim while displaying  updates then continue to next item
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display trim direction then continue to next item

Observability: TARS must display autopilot mode changes to the pilot.
Predictability: TARS should indicate upcoming autopilot adjustments.
Directability: Pilot can override TARS autopilot settings via voice command.
Information Requirement: Current airspeed, target speed, engine status.
Callout: Arming Speed mode to FLC {V_ENR} knots
TARS Performer Role: TARS will announce intention, wait 5 seconds, then arm SPD mode to FLC V2 then announce action done
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display V2 value and cleared altitude

Observability: Human sees TARS autopilot mode changes.
Predictability: Human anticipates TARS heading adjustments.
Directability: Human commands TARS via voice for heading changes.
Information Requirement: Current heading, target heading, engine status.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will announce intention, wait 5 seconds, then arm HDG mode to runway heading then announce action done
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display runway heading

Observability: Performer must callout the action. TARS displays autopilot status changes to pilot.
Predictability: Pilot anticipates TARS's autopilot engagement sequence.
Directability: Pilot can override TARS's autopilot actions via command.
Information Requirement: Engine status, autopilot mode, runway length remaining.
Callout: Will engage autopilot on your approval
TARS Performer Role: TARS will prompt and wait for approval to engage autopilot, then TARS will engage autopilot and announce action done

Observability: Human must see TARS's alarm status.
Directability: Human can confirm TARS's alarm callout via voice command.
Information Requirement: Engine status, alarm type, current flight phase.
Callout: Alarm {engine_failed_side} Engine Fire, low oil pressure {engine_failed_side} do you confirm?
TARS Performer Role: TARS will announce Alarm then continue to next item
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display alarm then continue to next item

Observability: Performer must callout safe altitude reached
Information Requirement: Current altitude, agreed safe altitude
TARS Performer Role: TARS will announce safe altitude reached then continue to next item
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display safe altitude value then continue to next item

Observability: TARS monitors pilot's throttle actions. TARS can help announce the side of the thrust lever and the action to conduct. The pilot must callout the action
Information Requirement: Engine status, throttle position, fire warning.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will announce affected engine side and alert if wrong throttle is adjusted during the task

Observability: The performer must callout the action, TARS must display the chrono status
Predictability: HUMAN should know when the timer is set off.
Directability: Pilot can override or redirect TARS actions via voice commands.
Information Requirement: Engine status, fire detection, extinguisher status, ATC communication status.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will announce countdown for TOP chrono then set the Chrono

Observability: TARS must display the chrono status and engine fire light status
Information Requirement: Engine fire status, checklist progress, TARS action log.
Callout: 15 seconds. If illuminated ENGINE FIRE Light is ON, Engine Fire Switch Lift Cover and Push
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will announce time's up

Observability: TARS monitors pilot's actions. HUMAN must callout the action
Information Requirement: Engine fire status, TARS communication readiness, ATC frequency.

Observability: TARS displays current altitude.
Information Requirement: Altitude, engine status, autopilot mode, ATC clearance.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will check safe altitude reached
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display safe altitude value

Observability: TARS displays airspeed and V2+10 status to pilot.
Information Requirement: Current airspeed, V2+10 target, engine status.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will check airspeed is sufficient and callout if it is not
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display target airspeed value

Information Requirement: Engine status, obstacle proximity, runway length, weather conditions.

Observability: TARS can support by calling out the action.
Information Requirement: Flap handle position, engine status, airspeed.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will callout the action

Information Requirement: V_ENR value, current airspeed
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display target airspeed value

Observability: TARS must display ATC communication status to the pilot.
Predictability: Pilot must anticipate TARS's next ATC message.
Directability: Pilot can override TARS's ATC communication with voice command.
Information Requirement: Engine status, ATC frequency, emergency checklist.
Callout: "Mayday, Mayday, Mayday,  Cessna Papa Oscar Lima Yankee, engine fire, {altitude} feet, continuing {heading} degrees heading, two souls on board."
TARS Performer Role: TARS will prompt and wait for approval to contact ATC to announce MAYDAY, TARS will display the message and frequency, then emit the message
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display a standard mayday message with current flight information

Information Requirement: Engine status, runway length remaining, ATC frequency.
Callout: "Cessna Papa Oscar Lima Yankee, cleared runway zero six left to land"
TARS Performer Role: TARS will readback the message.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display a written version of the ATC message

Observability: TARS displays current checklist step and expected worklow.
Predictability: TARS anticipates pilot's checklist step confirmations; pilot expects TARS to announce next steps.
Directability: Pilot can override or modify TARS actions via voice command. pilot confirms checklist initiation.
Information Requirement: Engine temperature, fire detection status, fuel shutoff valve position.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will announce starting checklist then proceed to start
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display next checklist and wait

Observability: Pilot confirms
Information Requirement: Engine temperature, pressure, fire detection status, checklist steps.

Observability: TARS can support the pilot by calling out the action
Information Requirement: Engine fire status, fuel boost switch position.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will announce affected engine side

Observability: TARS must display checklist progress and status to the pilot.
Information Requirement: Engine status, FIRE LIGHT status, checklist steps.
Callout: Thirty seconds if light remains on, {engine_failed_side} bottle discharge
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will announce time's up and failed engine side

Observability: The pilot must callout the action
Information Requirement: Engine fire status, BOTTLE ARMED switch position.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will sense and continue to next item

Observability: TARS monitors pilot's actions.
Information Requirement: FIRE WARN status, engine parameters, ATC frequency.
Callout: Turn Rotary Test to FIRE WARN
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will sense and continue to next item

Observability: TARS monitors pilot's actions.
Information Requirement: Engine fire status, TARS communication status.
Callout: Check IF both ENGINE FIRE lights ILLUMINATE
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will announce action

Observability: TARS displays checklist progress; pilot confirms task completion.
Predictability: TARS anticipates pilot's checklist initiation;
Directability: Pilot can postpone the execution
Information Requirement: Engine status, fire indicators, checklist steps, ATC communication status.
Callout: ENGINE FIRE checklist completed: Next checklist: Engine Failure / Precautionary Shutdown
TARS Performer Role: TARS will announce starting checklist then proceed to start
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display next checklist and wait

Observability: TARS must display ATC communication message to the pilot.
Predictability: Pilot must anticipate TARS's emergency declaration timing.
Directability: Pilot can override or confirm TARS's emergency declaration.
Information Requirement: Current aircraft status, ATC frequency, emergency nature.
Callout: Pan-Pan, Pan-Pan, Pan-Pan, Montreal Tower, Cessna Papa Oscar Lima Yankee, single engine operation after engine failure, {altitude} feet, {position_compared_to_initial}, {heading} degrees.  requesting vectors for immediate return to runway {RUNWAY_NUMBER}
TARS Performer Role: TARS will prompt and wait for approval to contact ATC to announce PANPAN, TARS will display the message and frequency, then emit the message
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display a standard panpan message with current flight information

Callout: Turning right heading one-five-zero, descending to three thousand, expect ILS runway zero-six left, C-POLY.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will readback the message.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display a written version of the ATC message

Callout: Will set heading to one-five-zero on your approval
TARS Performer Role: TARS will set heading as indicated by ATC upon pilot approval
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display heading as indicated by ATC

Callout: Will set altitude to three thousand on your approval
TARS Performer Role: TARS will set altitude as indicated by ATC upon pilot approval
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display altitude as indicated by ATC

Observability: TARS displays checklist progress and status updates to pilot.
Predictability: Pilot anticipates TARS's checklist sequence and timing.
Directability: Pilot can postpone checklist execution.
Information Requirement: Aircraft configuration, engine status, flight path, checklist items.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will announce starting checklist then proceed to start
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display next checklist and wait

Observability: TARS must display gear status.
Information Requirement: Gear status, airspeed, altitude.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will check L/G status and announce result

Observability: Human sees TARS' airspeed checks and status updates.
Information Requirement: Current airspeed, V2 speed, aircraft configuration.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will check airspeed status callout if too slow
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display airspeed target value

Information Requirement: Obstacle proximity, aircraft altitude, climb rate.

Observability: TARS must display flap status.
Predictability: Human must anticipate TARS's verbal reminders for flap retraction.
Information Requirement: Flap position, airspeed, altitude, and climb rate.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will check flaps status and announce result

Observability: TARS must display throttle detent status to pilot.
Information Requirement: Throttle position, climb rate, engine performance data.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will sense action and go to the next item

Observability: TARS displays yaw damper status changes to pilot.
Predictability: TARS anticipates pilot's yaw damper adjustments.
Directability: Pilot commands TARS to adjust yaw damper settings.
Information Requirement: Yaw damper status, aircraft attitude, flight phase.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will engage yaw damper if no engine failure upon pilot approval
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will give yaw damper usage suggestion

Observability: TARS displays anti-ice system status; pilot confirms visual indicators.
Predictability: TARS alerts pilot of potential anti-ice activation needs.
Information Requirement: Outside air temperature, wing surface status, system activation status.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will suggest action

Observability: TARS must display PAX SAFETY switch status to pilot and requirement.
Information Requirement: PAX SAFETY switch position, checklist status.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will suggest action

Observability: TARS must display landing light status to the pilot and requirement.
Predictability: Pilot must anticipate TARS's reminders for landing light status.
Information Requirement: Landing light switch position, current flight phase.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will suggest action

Observability: TARS displays pressurization status to pilot.
Predictability: TARS anticipates pilot's pressurization check initiation.
Information Requirement: Cabin altitude, differential pressure, system status.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will check cabin pressurization and announce result
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will remind normal pressure range

Observability: TARS displays altimeter setting changes; pilot confirms via cockpit display.
Predictability: TARS anticipates pilot's altimeter setting, pilot anticipates TARS altimeter setting
Directability: Pilot commands TARS to set altimeter to standard pressure.
Information Requirement: Current altitude, transition altitude, standard pressure setting.
Callout: Will set altimeter to Standard Pressure when reaching transition altitude = {TRANSITION_ALTITUDE} feet
TARS Performer Role: TARS will announce then set altimeters to STD upon reaching transition altitude
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display transition altitude value

Observability: Human must confirm crosscheck.
Information Requirement: Current altimeter setting, transition altitude.

Observability: TARS displays checklist status; pilot confirms task completion visually/audibly.
Predictability: TARS anticipates pilot's checklist completion cues; pilot expects TARS to announce.
Information Requirement: Checklist items, task status, aircraft configuration.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will announce current checklist completed

Observability: TARS displays checklist progress.
Predictability: TARS announces next checklist step before execution.
Directability: Pilot can override TARS actions via voice command.
Information Requirement: Engine status, autopilot mode, ATC communication status.
Callout: Next Checklist : ENGINE FAILURE / PRECAUTIONARY SHUTDOWN CHECKLIST
TARS Performer Role: TARS will announce next checklist then proceed to start
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display next checklist and wait

Observability: TARS displays checklist progress.
Predictability: TARS announces next checklist step before execution.
Directability: Pilot can override TARS actions via voice command.
Information Requirement: Engine status, fuel levels, system alerts.
Callout: Starting ENGINE FAILURE / PRECAUTIONARY SHUTDOWN CHECKLIST
TARS Performer Role: TARS will announce starting checklist then proceed to start
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display next checklist and wait

Observability: TARS displays engine status and monitor pilot's actions.
Information Requirement: Engine RPM, fuel flow, oil pressure, and temperature.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will check if affected engine throttle is cutoff
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display failed engine side

Directability: Pilot can override TARS actions; TARS prompts pilot for confirmation.
Information Requirement: Engine status, checklist steps, ATC communication status, autopilot engagement.
Callout:
"If possible, the engines should remain at idle for a minimum of two minutes prior to shutdown to allow the engine inter-turbine temperatures to stabilize and avoid turbine blade rub.
If the engine windmills for more than fifteen minutes without a positive indication of oil pressure, a notation is required in the engine logbook and the engine must be inspected in accordance with the
pratt and whitney engine maintenance manual.
If the engine windmills for more than fifteen minutes with the firewall shutoff closed or the boost pump turned off, the engine fuel pump must be inspected in accordance with the Pratt and Whitney Engine
Maintenance Manual."
TARS Performer Role: TARS will read the caution text
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display the caution text

Observability: TARS displays GEN switch status and monitor pilot's actions.
Information Requirement: GEN switch status, engine parameters, checklist steps.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will announce affected engine side

Observability: TARS displays IGN switch status and monitor pilot's actions.
Information Requirement: Ignition switch status, engine performance data.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will announce affected engine side

Observability: TARS must display current electrical load and recommendations.
Predictability: Human must anticipate TARS's suggestions for load reduction.
Directability: Human can command TARS to suggest specific load reduction actions.
Information Requirement: Current electrical load, available systems to shed, load reduction targets.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display current electrical load and safe limit

Observability: TARS must display fuel transfer status to pilot.
Information Requirement: Fuel levels, transfer knob position, engine status.
TARS Performer Role: TARS will check the fuel quantity and set fuel transfer to balance if necessary
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will suggest action

Observability: Human sees TARS status on ENGINE FIRE switch engagement.
Predictability: Human anticipates TARS will verify switch engagement.
Directability: Human can override TARS actions via verbal command.
Information Requirement: ENGINE FIRE switch status, engine parameters.
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will announce affected engine side

Observability: TARS displays checklist progress.
Predictability: TARS anticipates pilot's checklist initiation.
Directability: Pilot can command TARS to adjust checklist priorities; TARS suggests checklist priorities.
Information Requirement: Engine status, checklist steps, autopilot settings, ATC communications.
Callout: Checklist finished, Next checklist Single-Engine approach and landing
TARS Performer Role: TARS will announce next checklist then proceed to start
TARS Supporter Role: TARS will display next checklist and wait
